title: Toast
description: Transient feedback for confirmations, status, and errors. Compose inline with ToastComponent for demos and documentation, or push notifications through ToastService while ToastContainerComponent anchors stacks to a screen corner.
intro: Transient feedback for confirmations, warnings, and errors. Use `ToastComponent` for inline previews and layouts, and pair `ToastService` with `ToastContainerComponent` for stacked notifications from application logic.

featureSections.title: Toast service and container
featureSections.description: Primary and secondary via add(), plus helper shortcuts (info, success, warning, error). Reset clears the queue after manual dismissals. One ToastContainer per corner renders the shared ToastService queue.

featureSections.title: Basic toast
featureSections.description: Title and message with primary variant and filled appearance; Reset restores the toast after dismiss.

featureSections.title: Variants
featureSections.description: Full semantic set including primary and secondary for hierarchy, plus info, success, warning, and danger. Icons follow the variant unless you hide them.

featureSections.title: Appearance
featureSections.description: Surface weight from filled through outline, subtle, tint, and transparent using the same semantic variant.

featureSections.title: Sizes
featureSections.description: Scale density from small inline hints to large banner-style feedback.

featureSections.title: Dismiss, icon, and progress
featureSections.description: Control the close affordance, optional leading icon, and the timed progress strip tied to duration.

featureSections.title: Sticky (ToastService)
featureSections.description: When ToastMessage.sticky is true, automatic removal after duration is skipped-use for confirmations users must read, then dismiss or call clear(). Uses the same queue as above; no second ToastContainer.

featureSections.title: Custom content and actions
featureSections.description: Four NgTemplates mirror rich body content (default slot) and footer actions (slot actions). Each button enqueues the same composition via ToastMessage.contentTemplate and ToastService.add-use the ToastContainer from the Toast service section above. Reset clears the queue.

accessibility.title: Role and live region
accessibility.body:
The root uses `role="alert"`. `aria-live` is `assertive` for the danger variant and `polite` for others so assistive technologies prioritize errors without shouting over every hint.

| Variant | Live region |
| --- | --- |
| `danger` | `assertive` |
| all other variants | `polite` |

accessibility.title: Keyboard and actions
accessibility.body:
Toast itself is not a focus trap. Keyboard interaction depends on whether dismiss and action buttons are rendered.

| Element | Keyboard behavior |
| --- | --- |
| dismiss button | standard button keyboard support |
| projected actions | standard keyboard support from the projected control |
| non-interactive toast body | announced through the live region rather than focused |

accessibility.title: Dismiss and progress
accessibility.body:
When `dismissible` is true, the dismiss control is a `ButtonComponent`; keep toast copy concise so the name and message remain the primary announcement.

The progress strip is decorative timing feedback. Critical status should still appear in `title` and `message` instead of relying on the countdown bar.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Primary heading announced with the toast.
Supporting body copy; may be empty for title-only feedback.
Semantic palette (primary, secondary, success, warning, danger, info).
Surface treatment shared with buttons and banners.
Milliseconds for progress styling; ToastService applies auto-removal separately when used.
Shows the dismiss icon button when true.
Controls the leading Fluent icon derived from variant.
When false, adds toast--no-progress and hides the bottom timing strip (use with dense or action-heavy toasts). When true and duration > 0, animates the strip.
Corner hint on the component; stacks still rely on ToastContainer placement in real apps.
Two-way visibility; dismiss sets false and emits dismiss.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emitted after the user closes the toast or programmatic dismissal.
Hook for custom actions supplied through projected content (optional pattern).

paragraphs: Default projection fills toast__slot. Elements with slot="actions" render in toast__footer below the body so they stay clear of the dismiss control and the optional progress strip at the bottom edge.
blocks.title: Notes

apiSections.title: ToastContainerComponent - ui-toast-container
apiSections.lead: Subscribes to ToastService.toasts and renders each entry as ui-toast. Place one container per viewport corner (or region) you want stacked toasts.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Viewport corner / edge for the stacked list wrapper.

paragraphs: Use separate containers if you intentionally split toast stacks (for example one host per drawer); each instance reads the same service signal.
blocks.title: Notes

apiSections.title: ToastService
apiSections.lead: Injectable (providedIn: 'root'). Holds a reactive queue of ToastMessage items and schedules removal when duration is set and sticky is false.

blocks.title: Methods
rows: Adds a success toast; merges Partial<ToastMessage> overrides.
Low-level enqueue with full ToastMessage shape; assigns id; schedules removal unless sticky.
Removes a toast by id from the queue.
Removes using the message reference when id is present.
Snapshot helper around the current signal value.

paragraphs: Convenience methods delegate to add with a preset variant. The optional options argument accepts Partial<ToastMessage> to override duration, appearance, sticky, and other fields.
clear empties the entire queue - useful after users manually dismiss several toasts and you want a predictable reset state in demos or tests.
blocks.title: Notes

paragraphs: UI code should treat toasts as the source of truth for rendered stacks; getToasts() is a snapshot helper for diagnostics or unit tests.
blocks.title: Notes

apiSections.title: ToastMessage
apiSections.lead: Payload for ToastService.add and the implicit helpers. Title and message are required strings; other fields override defaults when rendering through the container.

blocks.title: Models
rows: Body text; may be an empty string.
Auto-dismiss timeout for ToastService; defaults are applied inside helpers.
When true, ToastService skips automatic removal after duration.
Optional stable id; generated when omitted.
Overrides semantic palette when rendering through the container.
Hide or show the leading icon.
Controls dismiss affordance for service-driven instances.
Controls the progress strip for container-rendered toasts.
Optional template rendered in the default projection slot below title/message (ToastContainer only). Define with ng-template in the host and pass the TemplateRef into ToastService.add.

paragraphs: Fields omitted when calling helpers pick up defaults inside ToastContainerComponent (for example icon visibility and dismiss behavior).
blocks.title: Notes

apiSections.title: ToastPosition
apiSections.lead: String union for anchoring a container. Match the value on ToastContainerComponent (and the optional position input on ToastComponent when you coordinate manual layout).

blocks.title: Related mechanisms
items: ButtonComponent - dismiss control and any slot="actions" actions you project.
IconComponent - leading glyph derived from ToastComponent variant when showIcon is true.
Shared tokens - Variant, Appearance, and Size align toast styling with buttons and other surfaces.
